operating_state: Total production reached 1,747 MBOED in Q1 2022, increasing 220 MBOED from prior year driven by Shell Permian acquisition, new wells online across Lower 48 unconventional plays, and absence of Winter Storm Uri impacts. Realized commodity prices surged with total average price reaching $76.99 per BOE versus $45.36 in Q1 2021, driven by Russian invasion of Ukraine and OPEC supply restraint.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased aligned with higher production volumes, while purchased commodities costs rose significantly due to elevated crude and gas prices. DD&A decreased despite higher volumes due to lower rates from price-related reserve revisions. ConocoPhillips allocated capital for energy transition activities addressing emissions reduction projects and low-carbon technology opportunities.
material_events: Completed $1.4 billion acquisition of additional 10% interest in APLNG increasing ownership to 47.5% and executed debt refinancing reducing total debt by $1.2 billion. Divested Indonesia assets for $731 million and completed CVE share monetization generating $1.4 billion proceeds. Distributed $2.3 billion to shareholders through ordinary dividend, VROC, and share repurchases while announcing increase in 2022 expected capital returns to $10 billion total.
macro_exposures: Commodity price volatility intensified due to Russian invasion of Ukraine with Brent averaging $101.40 per barrel versus $60.90 in Q1 2021. Company maintains unhedged position while monitoring Ukraine conflict impacts, OPEC supply updates, global demand fluctuations, inflation, and supply chain disruptions. Energy transition dynamics continue evolving with exposure to foreign exchange fluctuations across global operations.
forward_outlook: Second quarter 2022 production expected at 1.67-1.73 MMBOED reflecting seasonal turnarounds and weather impacts, with full-year guidance approximately 1.76 MMBOED. Operating capital guidance increased to $7.8 billion from $7.2 billion reflecting inflationary impacts. Management committed to using scenario analyses including carbon costs for capital allocation decisions while targeting asset dispositions by year-end 2023.
uncertainty_profile: Environmental accruals total $184 million for remediation activities with CERCLA sites identified and climate change regulations continuing to evolve with potential material impacts. Legal proceedings ongoing involving royalty payments, environmental damages, and climate change claims. Integration risks persist from Shell asset acquisition and collection risks remain from Venezuelan government obligations.
